channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: StepFun Reportedly Restructures for Potential Hong Kong IPO
item.description: Chinese AI startup StepFun is reportedly restructuring for a Hong Kong IPO, following multiple funding rounds that value the company at up to $6 billion.

item.title: Alibaba Launches Meoo AI Development Tool Targeting Non-Technical Users
item.description: Alibaba’s Meoo enables non-technical users to build full-stack applications via natural language, integrating multiple AI models and one-click cloud deployment.

item.title: Cloudflare One Client registration issues
item.description: Apr 16, 00:27 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 15, 21:55 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Apr 15, 21:26 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Apr 15, 21:03 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to...

item.title: Elevated Error Rate for Durable Objects and R2 Buckets in the APAC region
item.description: Apr 15, 20:47 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 15, 17:43 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Apr 15, 17:33 UTCInvestigating - Customers with R2 data or Durable Objects in APAC may experience high latency or elevated error rates.

item.title: Network Performance issues - Increased HTTP 5xx Errors
item.description: Apr 15, 19:27 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Apr 15, 19:14 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to investigate this issue.Apr 15, 19:13 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating an increased level of HTTP 5xx errors. We are working to analyse and mitigate...

item.title: ChatGPT FedRAMP workspaces not loading
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components FedRAMP (Operational)

item.title: My bets on open models, mid-2026
item.description: What I expect to come next and why, focused on the open-closed gap.

item.title: n8n Webhooks Abused Since October 2025 to Deliver Malware via Phishing Emails
item.description: Threat actors have been observed weaponizing n8n, a popular artificial intelligence (AI) workflow automation platform, to facilitate sophisticated phishing campaigns and deliver malicious payloads or fingerprint devices by sending automated emails. "By leveraging trusted...

item.title: Sweden Blames Pro-Russian Group for Cyberattack Last Year on Its Energy Infrastructure
item.description: In what was Sweden’s first public mention of the attack, the country’s minister for civil defense said it targeted a heating plant in western Sweden. The post Sweden Blames Pro-Russian Group for Cyberattack Last Year on Its Energy Infrastructure appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude.ai, API, Claude Code
item.description: Apr 15, 16:29 UTCUpdate - We have seen success rates for login to Claude.ai, including via Claude Code, stabilize and are working to fully resolve this issue. We will provide an update shortly.Apr 15, 15:40 UTCUpdate - Claude.ai and Platform are down. Login for Claude Code does not work via...

item.title: Cloudflare Billing Issues
item.description: Apr 15, 16:23 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 15, 14:19 UTCIdentified - Cloudflare is experiencing problems in processing changes through our billing subscription service for Argo Smart Routing. All other services are operating normally. We will provide an update once billing...

item.title: Cloudflare Workers API Latency
item.description: Apr 15, 16:07 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 15, 15:29 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Apr 15, 14:37 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating an increased latency when interacting with Workers Builds via webhooks from git...

item.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: the next generation of expressive AI speech
item.description: Our newest audio model introduces granular audio tags that give you precise control to direct AI speech for expressive audio generation.

item.title: Exploited Vulnerability Exposes Nginx Servers to Hacking
item.description: Hackers are exploiting CVE-2026-33032, a critical remote takeover vulnerability in the Nginx UI management tool. The post Exploited Vulnerability Exposes Nginx Servers to Hacking appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Capsule Security Emerges From Stealth With $7 Million in Funding
item.description: The Israeli startup aims to secure AI agents at runtime, continuously monitoring their behavior to prevent unsafe actions. The post Capsule Security Emerges From Stealth With $7 Million in Funding appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: ‘By Design’ Flaw in MCP Could Enable Widespread AI Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: Researchers warn that a flaw in Anthropic’s Model Context Protocol allows unsanitized commands to execute silently, enabling full system compromise across widely used AI environments. The post ‘By Design’ Flaw in MCP Could Enable Widespread AI Supply Chain Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: 100 Chrome Extensions Steal User Data, Create Backdoor
item.description: Published through five accounts, the extensions appear part of a coordinated campaign based on shared C&C infrastructure. The post 100 Chrome Extensions Steal User Data, Create Backdoor appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Actively Exploited nginx-ui Flaw (CVE-2026-33032) Enables Full Nginx Server Takeover
item.description: A recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting nginx-ui, an open-source, web-based Nginx management tool, has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-33032 (CVSS score: 9.8), an authentication bypass vulnerability that enables threat actors...

item.title: April Patch Tuesday Fixes Critical Flaws Across SAP, Adobe, Microsoft, Fortinet, and More
item.description: A number of critical vulnerabilities impacting products from Adobe, Fortinet, Microsoft, and SAP have taken center stage in April's Patch Tuesday releases. Topping the list is an SQL injection vulnerability impacting SAP Business Planning and Consolidation and SAP Business...

item.title: The Download: NASA’s nuclear spacecraft and unveiling our AI 10
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. NASA is building the first nuclear reactor-powered interplanetary spacecraft. How will it work?  Just before Artemis II began its historic...

item.title: Inside VAKRA: Reasoning, Tool Use, and Failure Modes of Agents
Retaining defensive advantage in the age of frontier AI cyber capabilities
item.description: As AI accelerates vulnerability discovery, organisations must raise their security baselines to safeguard their cyber security.

item.title: Deterministic + Agentic AI: The Architecture Exposure Validation Requires
item.description: Few technologies have moved from experimentation to boardroom mandate as quickly as AI. Across industries, leadership teams have embraced its broader potential, and boards, investors, and executives are already pushing organizations to adopt it across operational and security...

item.title: Cyberscammers are bypassing banks’ security with illicit tools sold on Telegram
item.description: From inside a money-laundering center in Cambodia, an employee opens a popular Vietnamese banking app on his phone. The app asks him to upload a photo associated with the account, so he clicks on a picture of a 30-something Asian man. Next, the app requests to open the camera for a video “liveness”...

item.title: Meet HoloTab by HCompany. Your AI browser companion.
No one’s sure if synthetic mirror life will kill us all
item.description: For four days in February 2019, some 30 synthetic biologists and ethicists hunkered down at a conference center in Northern Virginia to brainstorm high-risk, cutting-­edge, irresistibly exciting ideas that the National Science Foundation should fund. By the end of the meeting, they’d landed on a...

item.title: Microsoft Issues Patches for SharePoint Zero-Day and 168 Other New Vulnerabilities
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday released updates to address a record 169 security flaws across its product portfolio, including one vulnerability that has been actively exploited in the wild. Of these 169 vulnerabilities, 157 are rated Important, eight are rated Critical, three...

item.title: Luminous Ventures Closes USD 700 Million+ Dual-Currency Funds
item.description: Luminous Ventures has raised over USD 700 million across new USD and RMB funds, marking China‘s largest VC fundraise of 2026 so far, with LPs including sovereign wealth funds and global tech corporates.

item.title: Bota Biosciences Launches SAION AI Platform for Bio-Manufacturing
item.description: Bota Biosciences has launched SAION AI, a physical AI platform that connects cognition, orchestration, and execution to automate bio-manufacturing, reducing strain development from years to months.

item.title: Delta Intelligence Raises USD 14 Million+ to Build Humanoid Foundation Models
item.description: Delta Intelligence has raised over USD 14 million to build humanoid foundation models combining a proprietary 3D world engine, enabling full-body loco-manipulation for industrial and real-world tasks.

item.title: Building trust in the AI era with privacy-led UX
item.description: The practice of privacy-led user experience (UX) is a design philosophy that treats transparency around data collection and usage as an integral part of the customer relationship. An undertapped opportunity in digital marketing, privacy-led UX treats user consent not as a tick-box compliance...

item.title: The Non-Optimality of Scientific Knowledge: Path Dependence, Lock-In, and The Local Minimum Trap
item.description: arXiv:2604.11828v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Science is widely regarded as humanity's most reliable method for uncovering truths about the natural world. Yet the \emph{trajectory} of scientific discovery is rarely examined as an optimization problem in its own right. This paper argues that the...

item.title: Self-Monitoring Benefits from Structural Integration: Lessons from Metacognition in Continuous-Time Multi-Timescale Agents
item.description: arXiv:2604.11914v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Self-monitoring capabilities -- metacognition, self-prediction, and subjective duration -- are often proposed as useful additions to reinforcement learning agents. But do they actually help? We investigate this question in a continuous-time...

item.title: GoodPoint: Learning Constructive Scientific Paper Feedback from Author Responses
item.description: arXiv:2604.11924v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While LLMs hold significant potential to transform scientific research, we advocate for their use to augment and empower researchers rather than to automate research without human oversight. To this end, we study constructive feedback generation, the...

item.title: Narrative-Driven Paper-to-Slide Generation via ArcDeck
item.description: arXiv:2604.11969v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce ArcDeck, a multi-agent framework that formulates paper-to-slide generation as a structured narrative reconstruction task. Unlike existing methods that directly summarize raw text into slides, ArcDeck explicitly models the source paper's...

item.title: The Long-Horizon Task Mirage? Diagnosing Where and Why Agentic Systems Break
item.description: arXiv:2604.11978v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents perform strongly on short- and mid-horizon tasks, but often break down on long-horizon tasks that require extended, interdependent action sequences. Despite rapid progress in agentic systems, these long-horizon...

item.title: Uncertainty Quantification in CNN Through the Bootstrap of Convex Neural Networks
item.description: arXiv:2604.11833v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite the popularity of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), the problem of uncertainty quantification (UQ) of CNN has been largely overlooked. Lack of efficient UQ tools severely limits the application of CNN in certain areas, such as medicine,...

item.title: Schema-Adaptive Tabular Representation Learning with LLMs for Generalizable Multimodal Clinical Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2604.11835v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Machine learning for tabular data remains constrained by poor schema generalization, a challenge rooted in the lack of semantic understanding of structured variables. This challenge is particularly acute in domains like clinical medicine, where...

item.title: A Layer-wise Analysis of Supervised Fine-Tuning
item.description: arXiv:2604.11838v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While critical for alignment, Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) incurs the risk of catastrophic forgetting, yet the layer-wise emergence of instruction-following capabilities remains elusive. We investigate this mechanism via a comprehensive analysis...

item.title: When Reasoning Models Hurt Behavioral Simulation: A Solver-Sampler Mismatch in Multi-Agent LLM Negotiation
item.description: arXiv:2604.11840v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models are increasingly used as agents in social, economic, and policy simulations. A common assumption is that stronger reasoning should improve simulation fidelity. We argue that this assumption can fail when the objective is not to...

item.title: Polynomial Expansion Rank Adaptation: Enhancing Low-Rank Fine-Tuning with High-Order Interactions
item.description: arXiv:2604.11841v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Low-rank adaptation (LoRA) is a widely used strategy for efficient fine-tuning of large language models (LLMs), but its strictly linear structure fundamentally limits expressive capacity. The bilinear formulation of weight updates captures only...

item.title: Filtered Reasoning Score: Evaluating Reasoning Quality on a Model's Most-Confident Traces
item.description: arXiv:2604.11996v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Should we trust Large Language Models (LLMs) with high accuracy? LLMs achieve high accuracy on reasoning benchmarks, but correctness alone does not reveal the quality of the reasoning used to produce it. This highlights a fundamental limitation of...

item.title: Self-Distillation Zero: Self-Revision Turns Binary Rewards into Dense Supervision
item.description: arXiv:2604.12002v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Current post-training methods in verifiable settings fall into two categories. Reinforcement learning (RLVR) relies on binary rewards, which are broadly applicable and powerful, but provide only sparse supervision during training. Distillation...

item.title: LLMs Struggle with Abstract Meaning Comprehension More Than Expected
item.description: arXiv:2604.12018v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Understanding abstract meanings is crucial for advanced language comprehension. Despite extensive research, abstract words remain challenging due to their non-concrete, high-level semantics. SemEval-2021 Task 4 (ReCAM) evaluates models' ability to...

item.title: Benchmarking Deflection and Hallucination in Large Vision-Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.12033v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) increasingly rely on retrieval to answer knowledge-intensive multimodal questions. Existing benchmarks overlook conflicts between visual and textual evidence and the importance of generating deflections (e.g.,...

item.title: Think Through Uncertainty: Improving Long-Form Generation Factuality via Reasoning Calibration
item.description: arXiv:2604.12046v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) often hallucinate in long-form generation. Existing approaches mainly improve factuality through post-hoc revision or reinforcement learning (RL) with correctness-based rewards, but they do not teach the model to estimate...

item.title: [AINews] Humanity's Last Gasp
item.description: a quiet day lets us reflect on work in the time of AI

item.title: Notion’s Token Town: 5 Rebuilds, 100+ Tools, MCP vs CLIs and the Software Factory Future — Simon Last & Sarah Sachs of Notion
item.description: Notion's cofounder and head of AI peel back the curtains to talk about finally shipping the Knowledge Work AI agents the world has been waiting for.

item.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
item.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its...

item.title: What I’ve been building: ATOM Report, post-training course, finishing my book, and ongoing research
item.description: What I've been up to!
